news.headline: Iraq’s new PM Ali al-Zaidi formally takes over
news.summary: Iraq’s new prime minister Ali al‑Zaidi has formally taken office in Baghdad, pledging reforms.

news.headline: Iran war live: Tehran plans tolls in Hormuz; Trump warns of ‘very bad time’
news.summary: Iran to reveal its plan for Strait of Hormuz soon as Israel attacks Lebanon and Gaza, killing and wounding dozens.

news.headline: Bolivia launches early-morning crackdown on roadblocks outside La Paz
news.summary: The government of Rodrigo Paz has sent about 3,500 soldiers and police to clear protesters demanding his resignation.

news.headline: Secret police and propaganda: The Chinese agents spying on expats in US
news.summary: Experts say two espionage cases in the US highlight China's attempts to peddle influence globally over the past decade.

news.headline: The girl group who've sold out venues and toured the world - without releasing a record
news.summary: The all-female supergroup has sold out venues across the UK and Ireland, toured stadiums with Ed Sheeran, and built a global following - all without releasing a debut record.

news.headline: Hezbollah drone strike videos show evolving tactics against Israel
news.summary: BBC Verify has examined dozens of videos of fibre-optic drone attacks carried out by the Lebanese armed group.

news.headline: Foreign tourists are falling out of love with Goa - here's why
news.summary: Foreign visitors to Goa have nearly halved from their pre-Covid peak even as domestic tourism is booming.

news.headline: Félicien Kabuga, Rwandan accused of financing genocide, has died
news.summary: The ethnic Hutu tycoon was indicted on charges of genocide and crimes against humanity for allegedly arming Hutu militias that massacred more than 800,000 mostly ethnic Tutsis in 1994.

news.headline: German police assault protesters at Nakba anniversary rally
news.summary: German police hit and pepper-spray protesters as thousands gathered in Berlin to mark the 78th anniversary of the Nakba.
